     Edit freely; this file is not regenerated. -->

# DECISION_LOG

## How to read this file

Each row is a decision the project has made. The four columns
hold *receipts*: a one- or two-sentence prose cell each. Detail
belongs in the linked build-sheet section, spec sheet, or run
artifact — not in the row itself.

The cell budget exists so the log stays a navigable receipt and
does not grow back into duplicated SPEC content. Newer rows are
expected to honor the budget; older rows may be grandfathered.

## How to add a row

1. Pick the next sequential ID (`D###`).
2. Write the four cells:
   - **Decision** — what we decided. Imperative, present tense.
   - **Context** — why we needed to decide. The tension we
     were resolving.
   - **Consequence** — what changes as a result. Cite the
     build-sheet section or run artifact.
   - **Revisit** — the conditions that would justify reopening
     this decision.
3. Cite the source (run artifact, spec sheet, or measurement).

## Decisions

| ID | Status | Decision | Context | Consequence | Revisit |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| D001 | accepted | Trailer is the **FT712S2-D** (ACG FasTrac Deluxe) — 7×12 single axle, 3,500 lb GVWR, single 3.5K electric-brake axle, 2"×4" main rails, rear ramp door. **VIN 7V0W11214TU444163** (serial 444163). | Model was mis-recorded across older notes (6×10, 7×12 tandem, FT714); needed the real model + VIN to anchor dimensions and the build. | Confirmed against the [factory work order](reference/wells-cargo-ft712s2-d-work-order.md). Actual interior 81"×157", overall ~16'0"; 2"×4" rails + 4× factory 5,000 lb D-rings available for anchoring. Re-cut E-track to actual dims. | Owner to weigh the built unit for real payload; a different unit/VIN reopens. |
| D002 | accepted | **AIO = LiTime 48V 3500W (5 kW returned)**; solar = **roof 2× LG455N2W-E6 in 2S (910 W)** through-bolted to the steel roof bows **+ deployable ground 2S pair → 2S2P (1820 W)** when camped; side-rail cantilever rejected; no external MPPT. Defer the truck charger; pre-wire 4 AWG + Anderson now. | Sheet verified: PV 60–145 V op (60–115 V rec), **4400 W / 50 A** → 2S2P comfortably in-spec; 2S mid-window at all temps (Vmpp 84/79/~71 V; Voc ≤ ~109 V cold). 3S never valid (Voc 149.7 V > 145 V). Idle <50/<30 W ECO; ~75 A max battery draw < the pack's 100 A. Panels ride lengthwise inside (83" > 81" width). | [3500W specs](reference/litime-48v-3500w-aio-specs.md) + [panel datasheet](reference/lg455n2w-e6-datasheet.md). **Commissioning: cap total charge ≤100 A** (unit does 120 A PV+AC). | Roof-only 910 W proves thin between deployments. |
| D003 | proposed | Climate: install neither HRV nor diesel heater for July — rough-in penetrations + CO/power strings now; one measured curbside awning window; ~12 ft manual lateral-arm awning through-bolted to the top rail. | Mild July nights + under-floor-exhaust fire/weight risk make heat a cold-weather item; the awning is the binding July deliverable. | Run completed (accept-with-findings). See runs/juplaya-climate/. | Cold-weather mission, or measured window RO differs. |
| D004 | proposed | Interior: bikes side-by-side nose-forward on flush-recessed floor E-track; bed ~34"/shelf ~60"; 2×4-in-E-track-socket shoring; FRP-over-liner walls (no paint), XPS behind bed only; stowable RV step. | Reconfigurable bikes-out → sleeping-deck layout in the 81"×157"×78" interior. | **Floor-row spacing/position needs rework** — agy found ~9" handlebar interference at 24" centers and that the 2×4 steel runs at the perimeter, not off-centerline. See runs/juplaya-interior/. | After the tape-measure pass (bar width + steel locations). |
| D005 | accepted | Systems: fridge **PURCHASED — Dometic CFX3 95DZ** (95 L dual-zone, 12/24 VDC compressor; supersedes the ~50–55 L recommendation). Accessories order (keyed-alike Abloy locks, Trimax boot, LandAirSea 54, Blue Sea 5026, 12V/USB-C) stands. | DC-compressor-not-Midea held; owner sized up to dual-zone by purchase. 12/24 V auto-sensing → native on the 24 V house-bus lean. | Integration handed to the design-panel run: bus assignment (24 V native, 4.6 A), interior location for the 37.9×20.9×18.6" / 66.4 lb chest (+ lid-swing clearance), ~0.8–1.3 kWh/day vs solar; keep its spot under the 110 °F ambient ceiling. [Specs](reference/dometic-cfx3-95dz-specs.md). | Energy budget or interior fit forces a rethink. |
| D006 | proposed | **Single 24 V house bus**: one Victron Orion-Tr 48/24-16A isolated (remote on/off to cabin), Blue Sea 7443 20 A 80 V-class breaker on the 48 V side, Blue Sea 5026 as the 24 V block; **48→12 V converter deleted**; tow-vehicle 12 V stays isolated. | All house loads are 24 V-native/wide-range; the lighting bus is sunk; one converter = one quiescent draw. Owner's lean confirmed unanimously by the panel. | See the build sheet (juplaya-trailer-context.md), Power section (rail map + protection schedule + thermal derate). **Conditions:** 16 A sized for current loads (~12–15 A realistic worst case); a future 24 V diesel heater reopens sizing; cabinet venting must cover converter waste heat. | Future heater, new heavy 24 V loads, or converter thermal issues. |
| D007 | proposed | **Awning = Fiamma F45s 350 (06280B01R) on owner-fabricated steel riser brackets** at the curbside roof edge, case at/above the roofline; Tie Down S + lag/deadman anchors; wound-in discipline. Wall-mount dead (door geometry); flat-roof-adapter mount dead (panels own the roof width). | Panel interrogation killed both stock mounts: 5.35" case vs ~4.5" clear wall above the door; two 83×41" panels consume 82–83" of the 84" roof (radius corners shrink the flat field further). | See the build sheet, Climate/Awning section. **Pass/fail gates before fabrication:** door-trim height; top-plate/tube-post landing + backing; the measured roof drawing (panels+rails+bows+risers+door); deployed-fabric-vs-door at pitch; **required load-path outputs: bracket count/spacing, fastener size/grade, backing-plate span, documented wind+tie-down+vibration load case**. Fallback: freestanding shade. | Gates fail (→ freestanding), or measured roof changes the solar/awning split. |
| D008 | proposed | **Fridge integration**: the purchased CFX3 95DZ runs native 24 V (10 A fuse, 14 AWG) in a curbside bay immediately aft of the personnel door, E-track strapped. | 24 V-native on the D006 bus removes all conversion; bay placement keeps lid access (bikes-out) and outside reach under the awning. | See the build sheet, Systems + Interior sections. **Thermal gate:** ventilated/shaded bay away from the AIO/converter heat plume (110 °F ceiling; dual-zone cap >86 °F); verify lid-hinge orientation; ~1.0–1.3 kWh/day in the budget. | Bay overheats in July testing, or interior rework moves the bay. |
